ddls:
CREATE TABLE "bike_usage" (
  "usage_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each usage record */,
  "count" REAL /* Count of bike rentals for the day */,
  "season" INTEGER /* Season during which the data was collected (1-4) */,
  "holiday" INTEGER /* Indicates if the day is a holiday (1 = holiday, 0 = non-holiday) */,
  "workingday" INTEGER /* Indicates if the day is a working day (1 = working day, 0 = non-working day) */,
  "weather" INTEGER /* Weather condition (1-4 representing different conditions) */,
  "temp" REAL /* Temperature in Celsius */,
  "atemp" REAL /* Feeling temperature in Celsius */,
  "humidity" REAL /* Humidity percentage */,
  "windspeed" REAL /* Wind speed in m/s */,
  "year" INTEGER /* Year of the observation */,
  "month" INTEGER /* Month of the observation */,
  "day" INTEGER /* Day of the month */,
  "dayofweek" INTEGER /* Day of the week (0 = Sunday, 6 = Saturday) */,
  "hour" INTEGER /* Hour of the day (0-23) */,
  "station_id" INTEGER /* ID of the station where the bike was picked up and dropped off */,
  "start_time" TEXT /* Time when the bike was picked up */,
  "end_time" TEXT /* Time when the bike was dropped off */,
  "duration_minutes" INTEGER /* Duration of the bike usage in minutes */,
  "user_type" TEXT /* Type of user (e.g., regular, subscriber) */,
  "bike_id" INTEGER /* ID of the specific bike used */,
  "distance_traveled" REAL /* Distance traveled by the bike during the usage */,
  "route_id" INTEGER /* ID of the route taken by the bike */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("usage_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_bike_usage_holiday FOREIGN KEY ("holiday") REFERENCES holidays ("holiday_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_bike_usage_bike_id FOREIGN KEY ("bike_id") REFERENCES bikes ("bike_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_bike_usage_season FOREIGN KEY ("season") REFERENCES seasons ("season_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_bike_usage_route_id FOREIGN KEY ("route_id") REFERENCES routes ("route_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_bike_usage_weather FOREIGN KEY ("weather") REFERENCES weather_conditions ("weather_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_bike_usage_station_id FOREIGN KEY ("station_id") REFERENCES stations ("station_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "weather_conditions" (
  "weather_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each weather condition */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the weather condition */,
  "icon" TEXT /* Icon representing the weather condition */,
  "precipitation" REAL /* Amount of precipitation in millimeters */,
  "visibility" REAL /* Visibility in meters */,
  "uv_index" INTEGER /* UV index for the day */,
  "air_quality_index" INTEGER /* Air quality index for the day */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("weather_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "seasons" (
  "season_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each season */,
  "season_name" TEXT /* Name of the season (e.g., Winter, Spring) */,
  "start_date" TEXT /* Start date of the season */,
  "end_date" TEXT /* End date of the season */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("season_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "holidays" (
  "holiday_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each holiday */,
  "holiday_name" TEXT /* Name of the holiday */,
  "date" TEXT /* Date of the holiday */,
  "is_public_holiday" INTEGER /* Indicates if the holiday is a public holiday */,
  "is_school_holiday" INTEGER /* Indicates if the holiday is a school holiday */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("holiday_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "analysis_reports" (
  "report_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each report */,
  "report_date" TEXT /* Date the report was generated */,
  "generated_by" TEXT /* Name of the user who generated the report */,
  "report_data" TEXT /* Content of the report data */,
  "report_type" TEXT /* Type of report (e.g., daily, weekly, monthly) */,
  "report_status" TEXT /* Status of the report (e.g., draft, published) */,
  "report_url" TEXT /* URL to the report file if stored externally */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("report_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., admin, analyst) */,
  "phone_number" TEXT /* Phone number of the user */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Last login time of the user */,
  "is_active" INTEGER /* Boolean to indicate if the user is active */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Date and time when the user account was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Date and time when the user account was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the system */,
  "access_time" TEXT /* Date and time of access */,
  "action" TEXT /* Type of action performed (e.g., view, generate report) */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address of the user accessing the system */,
  "user_agent" TEXT /* User agent string of the device used to access the system */,
  "success" INTEGER /* Boolean to indicate if the access was successful */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "stations" (
  "station_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each station */,
  "station_name" TEXT /* Name of the station */,
  "location" TEXT /* Location of the station (latitude and longitude) */,
  "capacity" INTEGER /* Capacity of the station (number of bikes it can hold) */,
  "is_active" INTEGER /* Boolean to indicate if the station is active */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("station_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "bikes" (
  "bike_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each bike */,
  "bike_type" TEXT /* Type of bike (e.g., standard, electric) */,
  "last_maintenance" TEXT /* Date of the last maintenance */,
  "is_active" INTEGER /* Boolean to indicate if the bike is active */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("bike_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "routes" (
  "route_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each route */,
  "route_name" TEXT /* Name of the route */,
  "start_station_id" INTEGER /* ID of the starting station */,
  "end_station_id" INTEGER /* ID of the ending station */,
  "distance" REAL /* Distance of the route in kilometers */,
  "estimated_time" INTEGER /* Estimated time to travel the route in minutes */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("route_id")
)
